questions.explanation: ERP modules organize related processes and data (e.g., HR, Finance) so the organization can run end-to-end operations in an integrated way.

options.A: To group related business processes and data into a functional area
options.B: To replace the company’s internet connection
options.C: To store only scanned paper documents
options.D: To prevent any integration with other systems

questions.question: In an ERP system, what is the primary purpose of a 'module' (e.g., Finance, HR, Procurement)?

questions.explanation: An exclusive gateway routes the flow to exactly one of the outgoing paths based on conditions.

options.A: A point where all paths must run in parallel
options.B: A decision point where only one outgoing path is chosen
options.C: A database table used for reporting

questions.question: In BPMN, what does an 'Exclusive Gateway' (XOR) represent?

questions.explanation: Master data is the shared, relatively stable reference data (e.g., customer, vendor, employee, job roles) used across many processes.

options.A: Historical transaction records like invoices and payments
options.B: Stable core records used across processes, such as customers, vendors, and job positions
options.C: Temporary test data created only for UAT

questions.question: Which statement best describes 'master data' in an ERP context?

questions.explanation: LinkedIn is a widely used online job platform for job search, networking, and applications.
questions.question: Which is an example of an online job platform commonly used to search and apply for vacancies?

questions.explanation: Business rules and acceptance criteria belong in the functional requirements section so developers and testers can implement and validate the behavior.
questions.question: You are writing a functional specification for an ERP enhancement that flags candidates whose work authorization will expire within 60 days. Which section should clearly define the rule logic and acceptance criteria?

questions.explanation: Configuration uses standard settings (e.g., workflows, tolerances, org structures) to adapt the ERP without altering vendor core code.

options.A: Changing standard source code in the vendor core
options.B: Defining approval workflows and tolerance limits using system settings
options.C: Building a new database engine for the ERP

questions.question: In SAP S/4HANA or Oracle ERP Cloud, which activity is typically considered configuration (not custom development)?

questions.explanation: A LEFT JOIN with a NULL check on the right table finds orphan records where no matching employer exists.

options.A: SELECT c.* FROM candidate c INNER JOIN employer e ON c.employer_id = e.id;
options.B: SELECT c.* FROM candidate c WHERE c.employer_id IN (SELECT id FROM employer);
options.C: SELECT c.* FROM candidate c LEFT JOIN employer e ON c.employer_id = e.id WHERE e.id IS NULL;
options.D: SELECT * FROM employer WHERE id = NULL;

questions.question: You need to validate that every placed candidate has a corresponding employer record in the ERP after migration. Which SQL query best identifies candidates whose employer_id does NOT exist in the employer table?

questions.explanation: A controlled deduplication strategy with survivorship and legacy-to-new cross-references reduces duplicates and maintains auditability and traceability.

options.A: Delete all duplicates automatically and keep no record of what was removed
options.B: Use deterministic matching rules (e.g., normalized name + DOB + phone/email), assign a survivorship record, and store cross-reference mappings from legacy IDs to the new master ID
options.C: Ignore duplicates and let recruiters decide later without any system support
options.D: Migrate only the most recent 10% of records to avoid duplicates

questions.question: During data migration, you discover duplicate candidate profiles caused by different spellings and missing national IDs. Which approach best reduces duplicates while preserving traceability for audit purposes?

questions.explanation: Middleware enables transformation, validation, error handling, retries, and monitoring—key for stable EDI-to-ERP integrations.

options.A: Have recruiters manually retype applications into the ERP to avoid integration complexity
options.B: Send EDI directly into the ERP database tables with no validation
options.C: Use middleware to translate EDI to the ERP’s canonical format (e.g., IDoc/API payload), apply validation, and implement retry/error queues with monitoring
options.D: Convert EDI to PDF and email it to the ERP support team

questions.question: A partner job board sends candidate applications via EDI, while your ERP expects structured inbound messages. Which integration design is most robust for reliability and monitoring?

questions.explanation: When UAT reveals a compliance-impacting mismatch, the correct approach is to validate the legal requirement, update specs and test cases, retest, and secure formal approval to ensure traceable compliance.

options.A: Disable the rule in production immediately without documentation
options.B: Treat it as a requirements/legislation gap: confirm the legal exception with HR/legal, update the functional specification and test cases, then retest and obtain formal UAT sign-off
options.C: Ask developers to change the rule based only on verbal feedback and skip retesting to meet the deadline
options.D: Ignore the issue because UAT feedback is optional

questions.question: In UAT for an employment integration program, users report that a new 'placement eligibility' rule blocks candidates who should be eligible under labour legislation exceptions. What is the best next step to resolve this while protecting compliance?

topics_covered: Sap S/4Hana Or Oracle Erp Cloud (Platform Proficiency)
